rejected_readings.label: temporal_sphota
rejected_readings.rendering: The eternal word itself has real short, long, and prolated forms due to natural phonetic variations.
rejected_readings.why: Wrongly attributes temporal properties to the sphota itself. The commentary is explicit: vowel-quantity resides in the prakrta-dhvani (manifesting sound), not the eternal sphota. This reading commits a sort error by treating the manifestation's properties as properties of the absolute.

rejected_readings.label: no_figuration
rejected_readings.rendering: Time belongs equally to both the eternal word and its manifesting sound, with no figurative relation between them.
rejected_readings.why: Misses the technical point of upacaryate: the kala belongs to prakrta-dhvani literally, but only figuratively to sphota. This reading denies the figuration that rescues eternalism, collapsing the distinction between the manifested word and its eternal essence.

notes: The contract captures Bhartrhari's resolution of the tension between eternalism and phonological reality: temporal properties (time and vowel-quantity) belong literally to prakrta-dhvani (the manifesting sound); sphota is eternal but receives these properties only figuratively via upacaryate.
